description: Operating-system ops playbook as a tree: roots -> nodes -> leaves. A nested object is a node (its keys are the routes you can descend into); a list is a command leaf (runnable shell commands); a string is an answer leaf (plain text). Navigate with: /play <root> <node> ... and run a command leaf with /cmd <path>. Use single-word route names (paths are space-joined).

playbook.motd: welcome to the ops playbook bot
